title: Public opinion dynamics regarding Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter (X) in 2022

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Baseline sentiment prior to formal bid announcement
Estimated: Reaction to Musk's attempt to terminate the deal

events.description: Elon Musk reveals he has acquired a 9.2% stake in Twitter, becoming the company's largest shareholder. This disclosure signals his significant influence over the platform's future direction. | Twitter announces that Musk will not join the board of directors after initially being appointed. This reversal creates immediate public uncertainty regarding his intentions for the company.
Musk makes an unsolicited offer to purchase Twitter, initiating a high-profile hostile takeover attempt. The move forces a public debate over the platform's ownership and governance.

Twitter's board adopts a 'poison pill' strategy to defend against Musk's hostile takeover bid. This action highlights the deep institutional resistance to the acquisition. | Twitter's board unanimously accepts Musk's $44 billion buyout offer. The agreement marks a definitive shift in the company's trajectory and ownership status.
After months of legal disputes, Musk reverses his position and agrees to proceed with the acquisition at the original price. This move ends the immediate threat of a protracted court battle.
Court filings reveal that Musk is under federal investigation regarding the Twitter takeover. The news introduces significant legal and regulatory risk to the acquisition process.

agents.name: Elon Musk
agents.description: CEO of Tesla/SpaceX and acquirer of Twitter.

agents.name: Twitter Board of Directors
agents.description: Initially resistant, later accepted the acquisition offer.

agents.name: Twitter Power Users
agents.description: High-activity users concerned about moderation policies.

agents.name: General Public
agents.description: Average US citizens with varying levels of interest in tech news.

agents.name: Advertisers
agents.description: Corporate entities concerned about brand safety on the platform.

notes: Pro-side is defined as support for Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter. Against-side is defined as opposition to the acquisition or negative sentiment toward the deal. Data quality is mixed; early rounds are estimated based on news cycle sentiment, while final round is based on YouGov survey data.
